action.reasoning: Observation: Iter 9 achieved test balanced_accuracy 0.8019 (val 0.822) with convnext_tiny, mixup+cutmix, balanced sampler, and two-phase training (50+20 epochs). Few-shot 0.6606 still lags. Analysis: There is a val-test gap (0.822 vs 0.802), potentially due to small validation set. To improve tail classes, we can add a cosine classifier (feature+weight normalization) in the CRT phase for better separation, and apply post-hoc logit adjustment at inference (tau=0.3) to reduce head class bias. Extending representation to 80 epochs and CRT to 30 epochs with stronger mixup (alpha=1.0) may further boost representation. Decision: Implement cosine classifier head for CRT, post-hoc logit adjustment saved in checkpoint, train for 80+30 epochs, and submit eval.
